title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent · Go Build Room
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds constrained relational feed-service schema from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of constrained relational feed-service schema while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Relational Entities
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 8041 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Relational Entities.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Relational Entities, keep Feed Go Relational Entities connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Feed Go Relational Entities preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
One wide posts table is simpler and preserves every invariant. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
